topic: Does this survive a Scottish winter? Season-by-season care
voiceover_script: Scottish winter sunshine is brutal on solar. Here's what this system does about it. December sunshine drops to under an hour of usable sun a day — nowhere near enough to run anything. The honest answer: shut it down. Drain the pump and lines so frost can't split them, and bring the battery somewhere frost-free. Come spring, wipe the panel, reconnect the battery, flush the lines, and restart the service — the system comes back exactly as it left off. Nothing needs watering in a Scottish winter anyway — the system just waits for spring, same as the garden does. The full season-by-season care plan is in the build guide — link in bio.

Come spring, wipe the panel, reconnect the battery, flush the lines, and restart the service — the system comes back exactly as it left off.
Nothing needs watering in a Scottish winter anyway — the system just waits for spring, same as the garden does.
The full season-by-season care plan is in the build guide — link in bio.
